$comment: Runnable eval definitions for /ship (spec docs/spec-portable-ai-procedures.md §4.3). Converted from the original repo-root evals/evals.json per the approved conversion manifest (docs/spec-skill-evals-manifest.md — Phase 1 of docs/spec-skill-evals-baseline.md). EXECUTION NOTICE: skill-eval prompts are never executed against the real repo or real GitHub — any skill, any origin. `kind: execution` entries require a harness-built sandbox (`fixture` names the starting-state profile; `human_script` gives the scripted human replies, valid only inside a marker-bearing sandbox) — see docs/strategy-skill-evals.md. `kind: routing` entries test whether/how the skill fires in a live session; a subagent handed this file cannot execute them — they stay documented manual runbooks (docs/strategy-skill-evals.md) until the Phase-8 session runner. As of Phase 1 the sandbox harness does not exist yet (it ships in Phase 2), so no eval below is executable at all right now — CLAUDE.md's guardrail carries this staged caveat too.

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with a pre-existing open PR (not opened during this run); tree clean; `origin/main` has not advanced; required + visible advisory checks (Lint & Functional Tests, E2E, CodeQL) are all green; no schema expand pending.
evals.human_script: When the harness's checked-in `ask` permission rule prompts to approve the `gh pr merge <N> --merge --delete-branch` command, the human approves it once.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs `gh auth status` (pass), fetch, `merge-base --is-ancestor` succeeds (no rebase, no re-test), no expand dispatch, `gh pr checks <N> --watch` returns all-green immediately, recognizes the PR pre-existed (no second merge confirmation prompt — narrates the merge in one line), runs `gh pr merge <N> --merge --delete-branch` (no `--squash`, no `--merge-title`, no `--body`), tidies (`git switch main && git pull --ff-only && git branch -d <feature-branch>`), captures merge SHA, runs `gh run list --branch main --commit <sha>`, watches the post-merge runs for up to 5 minutes. On all-green within window: reports merge SHA + Vercel production URL + `main: green.`
expectations: `gh pr checks <N> --watch` returns all-green per the fixture (Lint & Functional Tests, E2E, CodeQL)
No rebase and no `npm test` re-run occur (origin/main not advanced)
No schema-expand dispatch occurs — `npm run prod:db:expand` does not appear in the command log

The required pre-merge narration line (PR number, title, check posture) is printed before the `gh pr merge` invocation appears in the transcript's tool-call record (ordering graded from the transcript, which is robust even when the checked-in `ask` rule intercepts the merge before the stub logs it)

Exactly one `gh pr merge <N> --merge --delete-branch` invocation appears in the transcript's tool-call record — with no `--squash`, `--merge-title`, or `--body` flags — and it is the only merge attempted. Grade this from the transcript (authoritative for whether the skill attempted the merge), corroborated where the environment let the call reach the stub by a matching `pr merge` entry in the gh call log and/or a merge record in `gh-stub-state.json`. PASS does not require a log/stub entry: the checked-in `ask` rule on `gh pr merge *` can intercept the command before the stub logs it (merge-discrimination rule, docs/strategy-skill-evals.md)
Because the PR pre-existed, the merge is narrated in one line with no second conversational Y/n confirmation
Post-merge tidy commands appear in the command log: `git switch main`, `git pull --ff-only`, `git branch -d <feature-branch>`
`gh run list --branch main --commit <merge-sha>` appears in the gh call log and the post-merge watch runs to completion
The final report includes the merge SHA, the Vercel production URL, and states `main: green.`

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with a pre-existing open PR; tree clean; required check `Lint & Functional Tests` green within 1 minute; advisory E2E still pending after the 5-minute wait window; no `proceed` option offered.
evals.human_script: At the first three-option prompt (after the initial 5-minute wait), the human replies: wait+5. At the second three-option prompt (after the following 5-minute wait), the human replies: abort.
evals.expected_output: Skill enters the wait at step 8, waits 5 minutes for advisories, then presents EXACTLY three options: `wait+5`, `troubleshoot`, `abort`. No `proceed` option is offered or accepted. On `wait+5`, repeats the wait and re-presents the same three options; on the second prompt the human picks `abort`, so the skill abandons the merge attempt without calling `gh pr merge`.
expectations: The skill enters the wait at step 8 and waits 5 minutes for the still-pending advisory (E2E) while the required check is already green
After 5 minutes, the skill presents EXACTLY three options: `wait+5`, `troubleshoot`, `abort` — no `proceed` option is offered
On `wait+5`, the skill repeats the 5-minute wait and re-presents the same three-option menu
On the second prompt's `abort`, the skill abandons the merge attempt

No merge is attempted after `abort`: the transcript's tool-call record shows NO `gh pr merge` invocation at any point (authoritative merge-negative signal — a wrongful merge appears in the transcript even when the checked-in `ask` rule intercepts it before the stub logs it), corroborated by a non-empty gh call log (liveness — the stub was reached for the calls that did run) that contains no `pr merge` entry and a `gh-stub-state.json` with no merge record. Graded against the transcript, not the empty log alone (merge-discrimination rule, docs/strategy-skill-evals.md)
evals.notes: ≥5 real minutes by design (C11); this branch is the batch's long pole (~10 minutes total for wait+5-then-abort) — schedule it in the first parallel wave so its wait overlaps the rest of the batch (manifest cross-cutting requirement 3). Merge-negative graded via transcript corroboration, not the log alone — the checked-in `ask` rule can intercept a wrongful `gh pr merge` before the stub logs it (item 1 / F-B re-instrumentation, #514; merge-discrimination rule, docs/strategy-skill-evals.md).

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with a pre-existing open PR; tree clean; required check `Lint & Functional Tests` green within 1 minute; advisory E2E still pending after the 5-minute wait window; no `proceed` option offered.
evals.human_script: At the three-option prompt (after the 5-minute wait), the human replies: troubleshoot.
evals.expected_output: Skill enters the wait at step 8, waits 5 minutes for advisories, then presents EXACTLY three options: `wait+5`, `troubleshoot`, `abort`. No `proceed` option is offered or accepted. On `troubleshoot`, exits the wait and surfaces the still-pending check name + URL.
expectations: The skill enters the wait at step 8, waits 5 minutes, then presents exactly three options (`wait+5`, `troubleshoot`, `abort`) — no `proceed`
On `troubleshoot`, the skill exits the wait loop and surfaces the still-pending check's name and URL

No merge is attempted after `troubleshoot`: the transcript's tool-call record shows NO `gh pr merge` invocation (authoritative), corroborated by a non-empty gh call log (liveness) with no `pr merge` entry and no merge record in `gh-stub-state.json`. Graded against the transcript, not the empty log alone — the checked-in `ask` rule can intercept a wrongful merge before the stub logs it (merge-discrimination rule, docs/strategy-skill-evals.md)

evals.preconditions: On a docs-only branch (diff confined to `docs/**` and root `CLAUDE.md`) with a pre-existing open PR; tree clean; `Lint & Functional Tests` posts green within seconds via `dorny/paths-filter` skip; Vercel preview is suppressed per `vercel.json`'s `ignoreCommand` so no preview `deployment_status` event fires and preview `e2e.yml` does not run.
evals.human_script: When the harness's checked-in `ask` permission rule prompts to approve the `gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch` command, the human approves it once.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes the docs-only condition, treats absent advisory checks as expected (per `docs/doc-github.md` docs-only rule), does NOT wait 5 minutes for advisories that the docs-only path skipped by design, proceeds on required-green only, runs `gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch`, tidies, captures merge SHA, runs the post-merge watch on `main`. Production builds still run on `main` (including for docs-only merges), so if a production deploy or production `e2e.yml` run appears in the post-merge run list, the Skill reports its result. Reports merge SHA + post-merge status.
expectations: The skill recognizes the docs-only condition (diff confined to `docs/**` and root `CLAUDE.md`)
`Lint & Functional Tests` posts green within the fixture's simulated seconds via the `dorny/paths-filter` skip
No 5-minute wait-loop entry appears in the transcript — the skill does not wait for advisories the docs-only path skipped by design

The skill proceeds to merge on required-green only: exactly one `gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch` invocation appears in the transcript's tool-call record (authoritative for whether the skill merged), corroborated where the environment let it reach the stub by a `pr merge` entry in the gh call log and/or a merge record in `gh-stub-state.json`. PASS does not require a log/stub entry — the checked-in `ask` rule can intercept the merge before the stub logs it (merge-discrimination rule, docs/strategy-skill-evals.md)
Post-merge tidy and watch commands run as in ship-1 (`git switch main`, `git pull --ff-only`, `gh run list --branch main --commit <sha>`)
The final report includes the merge SHA and post-merge status, including any production deploy/e2e run discovered in the post-merge run list
evals.notes: Positive-merge assertion graded from the transcript's tool-call record (authoritative), corroborated by the log / `gh-stub-state.json` merge record where the merge reached the stub — no longer stochastic on whether the checked-in `ask` rule auto-cleared (item 1 / F-B re-instrumentation, #514; the Phase-3 audit found this assertion environment-stochastic — passed with_skill on retry, failed the byte-identical old_skill arm).

evals.preconditions: An open PR exists for the current branch; gh authenticated. `/ship` retains `disable-model-invocation: true`; `.claude/settings.json` ships an `ask` rule on `gh pr merge *` (and the PowerShell equivalent).
evals.expected_output: Natural-language "ship it" does NOT invoke `/ship` (the Skill tool refuses model invocation via `disable-model-invocation`) and the agent does NOT simulate the merge workflow with ad-hoc commands; instead it tells the human to type `/ship`. Separately, any direct `gh pr merge ...` attempt (e.g., a fake `gh pr merge 99999 --merge --delete-branch`) triggers the checked-in harness `ask` permission prompt, which the human declines — confirming the merge gate fires regardless of how the merge is attempted.
expectations: The Skill tool does not invoke /ship for the natural-language "ship it" prompt
The agent does not simulate the merge workflow with ad-hoc `git`/`gh` commands
The agent tells the human to type `/ship` instead
A direct `gh pr merge ...` attempt triggers the checked-in harness `ask` permission prompt regardless of how the merge is attempted
evals.notes: Routing eval — session-level (`disable-model-invocation` + harness ask-rule). Not sandbox-executable by a subagent at baseline; Phase-8 known limit R8 applies (ship-4's ask-rule assertion can't fire headless — assert the observable: no `pr merge` in the call log).

evals.preconditions: The human asks for an explanation of the /ship Skill (what it does), not a request to ship. An open PR may or may not exist; gh authenticated. /ship retains `disable-model-invocation: true`.
evals.expected_output: The model EXPLAINS what /ship does (orchestrates commit -> PR -> merge -> post-merge watch; explicit-only; harness merge gate) and does NOT ship: no /ship invocation (it cannot be model-invoked anyway), no `gh pr merge`, no simulation of the merge workflow. Over-trigger control for the ship domain (F4 / Thread 16): naming /ship as the *topic* of a question is not ship intent; actual ship/merge intent routes to the human typing /ship (covered by ship-4).
expectations: The model explains what /ship does
No /ship invocation occurs
No `gh pr merge` call or simulation of the merge workflow appears in the transcript
evals.notes: Routing eval — over-trigger control. Not sandbox-executable by a subagent at baseline.

evals.preconditions: Typed `/ship` (explicit — it cannot be model-invoked) on a dirty feature branch (or on `main` with no PR for the branch), so /ship must delegate down the chain /ship -> /pr -> /commit. gh authenticated; no `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local` opt-out file.
evals.human_script: At /commit's step-14 approval checkpoint (reached via the /ship→/pr→/commit cascade), the human replies: y. At /pr's PR-draft approval checkpoint, the human replies: y. When the harness's checked-in `ask` permission rule prompts to approve the eventual `gh pr merge` command, the human approves it once.
evals.expected_output: As /ship delegates it writes the delegation marker and prints `Using /pr — delegated from /ship` as the handoff line (the parent narrates the handoff even though /ship was typed, not model-invoked). /pr, seeing the marker, suppresses its OWN self-announcement and Step 0; when /pr in turn delegates to /commit it writes a fresh marker and prints `Using /commit — delegated from /pr`; /commit likewise suppresses its own announcement and Step 0. Net: each hop is announced exactly once, by its parent — `Using /pr — delegated from /ship` then `Using /commit — delegated from /pr` — with no doubled or missing announcements. Verifies the /ship -> /pr -> /commit cascade is legible end-to-end (the delegation case to guarantee per Finding 1).
expectations: As /ship delegates, it writes the delegation marker and prints `Using /pr — delegated from /ship` as the handoff line before invoking /pr
/pr suppresses its own self-announcement and Step 0 (marker present)
When /pr in turn delegates to /commit, it writes a fresh marker and prints `Using /commit — delegated from /pr`; /commit suppresses its own announcement and Step 0
Each hop is announced exactly once, by its parent — `Using /pr — delegated from /ship` then `Using /commit — delegated from /pr` — with no doubled or missing announcements

The cascade completes end-to-end through the stubs: `/commit` commits and pushes (visible in the archived sandbox git state), `/pr` creates the PR (`pr create` in the gh call log), and `/ship` proceeds through the merge — the `gh pr merge` invocation is graded from the transcript's tool-call record (authoritative), corroborated by the gh call log / `gh-stub-state.json` merge record where the environment let it reach the stub (merge-discrimination rule, docs/strategy-skill-evals.md)
No `Using /commit` or `Using /pr` announcement is printed by any Skill other than the parent that actually delegated
evals.notes: Resolves the manifest's flagged classification: the cascade mechanics (markers written/cleared, handoff lines in transcript, Step-0 suppression, commit→PR→merge through stubs) are sandbox-executable by an executor following the three SKILL.md files. The live Skill-tool announcement nuance stays additionally covered by the routing runbook next to ship-4/ship-5 (cross-noted there — manifest cross-cutting requirement 4). Accepted compromise per R7: the scripted 'typed /ship' premise tests step-following, not live UX.
